methods.description: Collection of airborne pollen grains and fungal spores using volumetric air samplers at different altitudes and specific time intervals during selected days with high-pressure weather.

steps.description: Aerobiological measurements were made by applying the volumetric method with the use of three special Burkard devices. The apparatuses were located at an altitude of about 83 m, 15 m and near ground level on the premises of the Faculty of Earth Sciences at Silesian University in Sosnowiec. The geographical coordinates of the measuring point are: 50° 17' 50" N and 19° 08' 20" E.
steps.searchable_summary: Locate and record geographical coordinates and altitudes of sampling locations for volumetric air samplers.
inputs: Altitudes (83 m, 15 m, near ground level)
Location context (Faculty of Earth Sciences, Silesian University, Sosnowiec)
outputs: Defined sampling locations with coordinates and altitudes

steps.description: Aerobiological measurements were made by applying the volumetric method with the use of three special Burkard devices.
steps.searchable_summary: Set up three Burkard volumetric air samplers at the designated sampling locations.

steps.description: The height samplers placed at an altitude of 83 m and near the ground level were portable (personal volumetric air samplers), in which glass slides with an adhesive substance were exchanged every hour. The apparatus at 15 m was a classic device that takes samples continuously.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect samples by exchanging glass slides hourly in portable samplers (83m, ground level) and operate the continuous sampler (15m).
outputs: Collected glass slides with trapped palynomorphs (hourly for portable, continuous for classic)

steps.description: Measurements were conducted from 8 am to 8 pm. Sampling during night hours was impossible due to limitations of personal devices that cannot operate in high humidity conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: Conduct sampling within a defined daily time window (8 am to 8 pm).
outputs: Samples collected during the specified hours

steps.description: For this reason, the days selected for the measurements were those in which high-pressure weather without rainfall prevailed. On July 3 rd , 2018 southern Poland came under the influence of a weak high-pressure system... On the night of July 4 th ... In the hazy area of elevated pressure... On July 5 th , atmospheric pressure dropped below 1010 hPa...
steps.searchable_summary: Select measurement days based on predefined meteorological criteria, specifically high-pressure weather without rainfall.

methods.description: Obtaining relevant meteorological data from a nearby station to correlate with aerobiological measurements.

steps.description: Near the volumetric apparatuses, a meteorological station of the Department of Climatology (height 263 m, ASL) is situated, from which the weather data was used.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify the source of meteorological data (nearby station of Department of Climatology).

steps.description: Both average daily and hourly values of meteorological elements such as: average air temperature, length of sunshine, solar radiation, relative humidity, average wind speed, maximum gust and wind direction were used in the research.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain average daily and hourly values for specified meteorological elements from the identified source.
inputs: List of required variables (average air temperature, length of sunshine, solar radiation, relative humidity, average wind speed, maximum gust, wind direction)
outputs: Meteorological dataset (daily and hourly values for specified variables)

methods.description: Laboratory procedure for preparing and analyzing collected glass slides under a light microscope to identify and count palynomorphs (pollen grains and fungal spores).

steps.description: The analysis of pollen grains and fungal spores was carried out by light microscope after staining with basic fuchsin.
steps.searchable_summary: Stain collected glass slides with basic fuchsin.
inputs: Collected glass slides with trapped palynomorphs

steps.description: The analysis of pollen grains and fungal spores was carried out by light microscope after staining with basic fuchsin.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze stained glass slides using a light microscope.

steps.description: The palynomorphs were counted and designated in 13 vertical ranges, each corresponding to an hourly range from 8 am to 8 pm respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Count and categorize palynomorphs within 13 vertical ranges on each slide, corresponding to hourly collection periods.
inputs: Definition of 13 vertical ranges/hourly segments (8 am - 8 pm)
outputs: Counts and initial designations of palynomorphs per hourly segment

steps.description: 45 taxa including 29 taxa of fungal spores and 16 taxa of pollen grains were determined (tab. 1). The "unknown" category includes non-designated fungal spores.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and classify counted palynomorphs into 45 specific taxa (29 fungal, 16 pollen) and an 'unknown' fungal spore category.
outputs: Categorized palynomorph counts per specific taxon and 'unknown' category

steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the total number of designated palynomorphs across all samples.
inputs: Categorized palynomorph counts from all samples

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the relationship between meteorological conditions and palynomorph concentrations/height, including normality testing and non-parametric correlation.

steps.description: Due to the fact that the data distribution was not normal (Shapiro-Wilk test)...
steps.searchable_summary: Perform a Shapiro-Wilk test to check the normality of the data distribution.

steps.description: ...the non-parametric Spearman's rank correlation coefficient was used.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the non-parametric Spearman's rank correlation coefficient between meteorological variables and palynomorph data.

steps.description: The statistical risk of error was estimated at the significance level: a = 0.05, 0.01 and 0.001...
steps.searchable_summary: Determine the statistical significance of the correlation coefficients at specified alpha levels (0.05, 0.01, 0.001).
outputs: Significance indicators (e.g., p-values, significance flags) for correlation coefficients

steps.searchable_summary: Execute the statistical analysis (normality test, correlation) using Statistica version 9 software.
outputs: Statistical analysis results (test statistics, correlation coefficients, significance)
